steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Apply cofunction identities
steps.content: Use the cofunction shortcut: $\cos(90 - a)^{\circ} = \sin a^{\circ}$ and $\sin(90 - b)^{\circ} = \cos b^{\circ}$. With $y = 0$, $\sin(a + 0)^{\circ} = \sin a^{\circ}$. The expression becomes: $$2\sin a^{\circ} \cdot \cos b^{\circ} + \sin a^{\circ} \cdot \cos b^{\circ}.$$

steps.title: Factor and substitute
steps.content: Factor: $3\sin a^{\circ} \cdot \cos b^{\circ}$. Substitute $\sin a^{\circ} = 0.50$ and $\cos b^{\circ} = 0.99$: $$3 \times 0.50 \times 0.99 = 1.485.$$ Therefore 1.485 is correct.
